current.summary: Provenance spec design: applied panel consensus (JSON canonicalization, split hashes, meta.yaml), resolved D1-D3, discussion points D4-D12 pending

activeWork.description: Designing provenance and attestation model for syllago content types; 6-persona panel discussion completed and consensus applied
activeWork.progress: Panel discussion completed. Applied three key decisions to spec: D1 (JSON canonicalization RFC 8785), split content_hash/meta_hash (not combined), universal meta.yaml (not type-specific). D3 (key distribution) addressed with GitHub Actions + Sigstore OIDC strategy (zero-friction signing for GitHub-hosted content). Open discussion points: D2 (file tree ordering), D4-D12 (sidecar placement, loadout provenance, agent authorship, identity binding, attack defense, fork versioning).

git.lastCommit: 3b34379 handoff: CDR execution complete, content-based classification gap identified

recentDecisions: D1 RESOLVED: RFC 8785 (JCS) for JSON canonicalization of metadata
D2 RESOLVED: Separate content_hash and meta_hash (metadata edits won't break content integrity)
D3 RESOLVED (partial): Universal meta.yaml across all content types; GitHub Actions + Sigstore OIDC eliminates manual key distribution for published content
Panel consensus: manifest approach correct (one .syllago/manifest.yaml not per-item files); SSH signing in v0.1 not v0.2
nextSteps: Finalize D2: Complete file tree ordering specification for hash consistency across all tools
Resolve D4: JSON-merge sidecar placement for hooks and MCP configs (Priya flagged security risk)
Resolve D5: Loadout provenance model — manifest-only hash vs full resolved content
Resolve D6-D8: Agent authorship rules, provenance fingerprint format, denouncement records
Resolve D9-D12: Missing meta.yaml handling, sidecar stripping attacks, human identity binding, fork version semantics
Integrate Sigstore findings: update spec with two-path signing (GitHub Actions via OIDC + local manual signing)
Create implementation reference (hash test vectors, canonicalization examples, manifest schema with examples)

context.notes: Panel (6 personas: Jordan/security, Marcus/implementation, Aisha/UX, Priya/attack surface, Sam/ecosystem, Casey/workflows) provided critical feedback. Unanimous on metadata canonicalization as blocker. Sigstore OIDC insight solves D3 for dominant use case (GitHub repos). Aisha's concern about metadata edits drove content/meta hash split. Spec in docs/spec/provenance.md covers 6 core decisions + 12 discussion points (D1-D12). D1-D3 have direction; D4-D12 still require design discussion. Next session should continue from D2 (file tree ordering) and work through remaining points before implementation phase.

previousSessions.summary: Extensive provenance research and adversarial panel review: hash algorithm options, sidecar patterns, manifest vs per-item organization, prior panel recommended 8-field minimum spec
completedWork: Prior adversarial review panel (14-adversarial-review-panel.md) and provenance deep dive research (15-provenance-deep-dive.md)
Identified core tension: .syllago.yaml per-item (clutter) vs central manifest (tracking)
nextSteps: Panel discussion on spec design with decision framework (COMPLETED this session)
